# @vllnt/convex-flags

> Feature flags as a Convex component - backend-evaluated, reactive kill-switches

`@vllnt/convex-flags` is a publishable Convex component targeting `convex@^1.36.1`.
It stores feature flags in its own sandboxed tables, keyed by an opaque host-chosen
string. A flag value is a typed primitive (`boolean | string | number`) and supports
attribute-targeting rules, weighted percentage rollouts with stable per-subject
bucketing, per-subject overrides, and reversible archiving. Flags are evaluated inside
Convex queries, so changes stream to connected clients over the same reactive
subscription mechanism as app data. The host owns auth and gates every management call.
An optional `./react` entry ships `useFlag`/`useFlags` hooks; `react` is an optional peer.

## Docs

- [README](README.md): Project overview, installation, usage examples, architecture
- [API Reference](docs/API.md): Public class methods, return shapes, input validation
- [Changelog](CHANGELOG.md): Version history and release notes
- [Contributing](CONTRIBUTING.md): Development setup, validation commands, release workflow

## Examples

- [Example component wrapper](example/convex/example.ts): Host-app wrapper functions
- [Example tests](example/convex/example.test.ts): End-to-end usage coverage with `convex-test`
